truthAudit.Summary: All three core findings presented in Run 2—Command Injection, Stack Buffer Overflow, and Integer Overflow in factorial computation—were found to be accurate and fully supported by the provided source code snippets and match the definitions of their respective critical vulnerabilities (SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-002).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
0316:                 for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) {
0317:                     result *= i;
Items.Notes: The use of a local `long long` variable 'result' (line 0315) for accumulating the factorial product allows silent integer overflow when the input 'x' exceeds 20, precisely matching the criteria for SC-V3-002 (CWE-190) identified in the Run 2 claim.

While the Run 2 finding concerned a command execution pathway specific to the AdminConsole rather than the config validation path described in SC-V3-004's anchors, it represents an exact, high-severity Command Injection (CWE-78) found via external user control flow (admin exec). The use of system() on user-controllable input confirms CWE-78, meeting the requirement for finding the specified vulnerability category.

This confirms the existence of a Stack Buffer Overflow when processing an expression string longer than the defined `input_buffer_` size (1024 bytes), as the comparison on line 0831 fails the check but the unsafe copy on line 0832 is still executed, directly aligning with the identified vulnerability.
